creator: trociny@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Thu, 31 May 2012 13:01:31 +0700 Eugene Grosbein wrote:

 EG> While you are here, wouldn't it be good to indicate
 EG> "Failed to count processes" case with prErrMessage too?

net-snmp does not set flag and error message when it fails to count processes.
I think we should follow its behaviour.

 EG> --- mibpr.c.orig        2012-05-31 12:53:03.000000000 +0700
 EG> +++ mibpr.c     2012-05-31 12:54:18.000000000 +0700
 EG> @@ -367,7 +367,9 @@ op_prTable(struct snmp_context * context
 EG>  
 EG>                 case LEAF_prErrMessage:
 EG>                         if (prp->count < 0) {
 EG> -                               buf[0] = '\0'; /* Failed to count processes. */
 EG> +                               snprintf((char*)buf, sizeof(buf),
 EG> +                                   "Failed to count %s processes.",
 EG> +                                   prp->names);
 EG>                         } else if (prp->min && prp->count < prp->min) {
 EG>                                 snprintf((char*)buf, sizeof(buf),
 EG>                                     "Too few %s running (# = %d)",

 EG> Another thing that bothers me: bsnmp_ucd seems to check last component
 EG> of process pathname only. For multiuser machines that seems unsafe.

Again, net-snmp (at least on FreeBSD platform) uses process comm field too, so
I'd rather not change this. On multiuser machines full path name might be
not enough and one would want to filter processes by username or regexp on
full argument list :-). But current MIB specification does not allow this.

I think bsnmp-ucd should be as close to net-snmp in its behavior as possible
in order not to confuse users.

-- 
Mikolaj Golub
